title: Postinstall migration: resync all sharedWith profiles on sweech upgrade
description:
When users `npm i -g sweech@latest` (or brew upgrade), the postinstall script should detect profile-config schema drift and proactively resync all profiles with `sharedWith` set. Right now upgrades leave existing profiles in whatever symlink state they were in at creation time, even if SHAREABLE_DIRS has grown.

Fix: add a `scripts/postinstall.js` that:
1. Reads ~/.sweech/config.json (skip if missing — fresh install)
2. Compares `lastResyncedSweechVersion` field (new) against current package.json version
3. If different (or absent), runs `sweech doctor --fix --quiet` and writes the new version back
4. Prints a single line: 'sweech: resynced N profiles after upgrade to vX.Y.Z' (don't block, don't prompt)

This is the install-time companion to T-076 (manual heal) and T-077 (invocation-time heal). Together they form a 3-layer recovery: postinstall (eager) → invocation (lazy) → doctor (manual).

context.text: The user discovered this when /vy-* skills were Unknown in a claude-pole session. Root cause traced to sweech profile dirs missing the commands/ + agents/ symlinks. The user has 16 sweech profiles — manually re-running doctor on each is a pit.

Done via /vy-go 2026-05-21. Added package-shipped scripts/postinstall.js, switched npm postinstall to that script, moved the upgrade marker into config.json as lastResyncedSweechVersion, kept install failures non-blocking with audit/lifecycle logging, and added focused postinstall tests. Proof: npm test -- --runInBand tests/postinstallHeal.test.ts tests/cliTypeAudit.test.ts passed 24 tests; npm test -- --runInBand passed 99 suites, 2387 tests, 5 skipped; npm run build passed; npx tsc --noEmit passed; npm audit --audit-level=moderate found 0 vulnerabilities; cd macos-menubar/SweechBar && swift build passed; git diff --check passed. E2E: isolated HOME postinstall smoke repaired a drifted sharedWith profile and printed the one-line resync message.

Adversarial follow-up: postinstall upgrade marker now writes ~/.sweech/config.json via atomicWriteFileSync instead of a direct write, preserving config integrity if the process is interrupted. Focused postinstall/cli-type tests, typecheck, build, and final Codex adversarial review passed.

acceptanceCriteria: postinstall script ships with the npm package
Detects version drift via .lastResyncedSweechVersion in config.json
Runs quietly unless there are repairs (then prints a one-liner)
Failures (e.g. permission denied) are logged to audit.log but never block the install
Brew formula calls the same script as a post_install hook
Test: bump SHAREABLE_DIRS, run postinstall, confirm broken profile is healed
